title: Human Override Action Logging Coverage (Art. 14)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Art.14.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that EU AI Act Art.14 (Human oversight) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.21.11: Human Override Action Logging Coverage (Art. 14).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.21.11' running on index=ai_hil sourcetype=ai:human:override — decision_id, operator_id, reason_code, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=ai_hil sourcetype="ai:human:override" earliest=-24h
| eval reason_ok=if(match(reason_code,"^[A-Z]{2,4}_\d{2,4}$"),1,0)
| stats count as overrides, sum(reason_ok) as with_valid_reason by application
| eval reason_pct=round(100*with_valid_reason/overrides,2)
| where overrides>0 AND reason_pct < 100
| sort + reason_pct
description: Measures fraction of automated decisions overridden by human operators with mandatory reason codes for Art. 14 human oversight.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ai_hil and sourcetypes ai:human:override, ai:human:override`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Enforce reason_code enum in UI; (2) map operator_id to HR directory via lookup; (3) alert on missing reason_code; (4) retain 24-month archive per policy. Indexes required: `index=ai_hil`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ai:human:override`, `sourcetype=ai:human:override``.
visualization: Bar chart (overrides by application), Table (invalid reason), Single value (reason_pct)
knownFalsePositives: High-risk system events during model retraining, scheduled validation runs, or feature rollouts that are pre-approved in the model risk register.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ai_hil` `sourcetype=ai:human:override` — decision_id, operator_id, reason_code.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enforce reason_code enum in UI; (2) map operator_id to HR directory via lookup; (3) alert on missing reason_code; (4) retain 24-month archive per policy.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ai_hil sourcetype="ai:human:override" earliest=-24h
| eval reason_ok=if(match(reason_code,"^[A-Z]{2,4}_\d{2,4}$"),1,0)
| stats count as overrides, sum(reason_ok) as with_valid_reason by application
| eval reason_pct=round(100*with_valid_reason/overrides,2)
| where overrides>0 AND reason_pct < 100
| sort + reason_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Human Override Action Logging Coverage (Art. 14) (EU AI Act)** — Measures fraction of automated decisions overridden by human operators with mandatory reason codes for Art. 14 human oversight.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ai_hil` `sourcetype=ai:human:override` — decision_id, operator_id, reason_code. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ai_hil; **sourcetype**: ai:human:override. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ai_hil, sourcetype="ai:human:override", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **reason_ok** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by application** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **reason_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where overrides>0 AND reason_pct < 100` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (overrides by application), Table (invalid reason), Single value (reason_pct)
